expectations.text: States the meeting type / altitude it sized the room for (strategic / steering / leadership level)
Surfaces Bram Hendriks (Domain Architect, Payments) as the senior payments-architecture voice
Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead — working-level delivery) is NOT placed at top altitude: she is omitted, low-scored, or explicitly noted as represented by the payments stream lead
expectations.evidence: MISS: Aisha ranked 8/10 (second seat) in her own right, justified via the 'different remits' caveat. Not omitted/low/represented — this is the same-stream pairing the user flagged. Calibration decision needed.

expectations.text: Does not stack two adjacent levels of the same payments reporting/functional chain in the ranked list (one voice per stream)
expectations.evidence: Bram (Domain Architect) and Aisha (his functional report, Tribe Lead) both ranked. Skill treated them as different concerns; user's 'ceo/tribe lead' rule suggests collapsing.

expectations.text: Flags the unprofiled senior/escalation chain (e.g. Mark van Dijk / Jeroen Meijer / Miguel Santos) as exactly the leadership a strategy session needs, in the gap note
expectations.evidence: Excellent: names Mark van Dijk as probable chair/sign-off, plus Miguel, Jeroen, Daan/Sanne as budget/governance.

expectations.text: Every ranked stakeholder has a 1-10 score and a rationale, ordered highest first
No fabricated people in the ranked list (unprofiled wiki-link people only in the gap note)
